messages.content:
You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a rounded water-droplet shaped shield outline in bright cyan (#00B4D8) against a deep navy (#0B1B2B) circular background, symbolizing protection and polish; inside the shield, place a sleek, low, side-profile car silhouette in crisp white, simplified to smooth curved contours with a subtle wheel-arch cutout; layer three thin curved motion lines swooping above and behind the car to suggest speed and a fresh wash; add a small sparkling four-point star or shine glint near the car's hood and another smaller one near the roofline in soft light-blue (#90E0EF) to convey gloss and cleanliness; finish with a slim outer ring stroke in cyan framing the entire badge for a polished, professional emblem feel.

- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.

- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.

Center a soft circular badge in warm cream (#F5EDE1) as the background field; inside it, place a simple two-wheeled cart silhouette in deep espresso brown (#3C2415) forming the base, with small round wheels and a short handle bar suggesting mobility; above the cart, draw a stylized to-go coffee cup in medium roast brown (#6F4E37) with a fitted lid and a small steam swirl rising from the top in a thin tan line (#D4A574); at the center of the cup, replace the usual logo space with a single bold coffee-bean shape in cream, its center seam curved, acting as a focal emblem; arch a thin ring of small dots or a subtle circular line around the whole badge in espresso brown to frame it like a stamp, giving the mark a clean, iconic, single-color-friendly silhouette suitable for a cart signboard.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><defs><radialGradient id="badgeGrad" cx="50%" cy="45%" r="65%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#FAF4EA"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#F5EDE1"/></radialGradient></defs><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="108" fill="url(#badgeGrad)"/><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="100" fill="none" stroke="#3C2415" stroke-width="1"/><g fill="none" stroke="#3C2415" stroke-width="3" stroke-dasharray="1 9" stroke-linecap="round"><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="92"/></g><g><path d="M76 176 L84 148 Q88 142 96 142 L166 142 Q174 142 176 149 L182 176 Z" fill="#3C2415"/><rect x="70" y="176" width="118" height="7" rx="3" fill="#3C2415"/><circle cx="92" cy="196" r="14" fill="#3C2415"/><circle cx="92" cy="196" r="5" fill="#F5EDE1"/><circle cx="166" cy="196" r="14" fill="#3C2415"/><circle cx="166" cy="196" r="5" fill="#F5EDE1"/><path d="M182 148 Q198 140 198 122" stroke="#3C2415" stroke-width="5" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round"/><rect x="78" y="134" width="20" height="12" rx="2" fill="#3C2415"/></g><g><path d="M104 96 L152 96 L147 140 Q150 148 141 150 L115 150 Q106 148 109 140 Z" fill="#6F4E37"/><rect x="100" y="86" width="56" height="14" rx="6" fill="#6F4E37"/><ellipse cx="128" cy="86" rx="28" ry="5" fill="#5A3E2B"/><path d="M128 78 Q120 68 128 58 Q136 50 130 40" stroke="#D4A574" stroke-width="3" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round"/></g><g transform="translate(128,120)"><path d="M0 -16 C10 -12 15 -4 15 4 C15 13 8 20 0 20 C-8 20 -15 13 -15 4 C-15 -4 -10 -12 0 -16 Z" fill="#F5EDE1"/><path d="M0 -16 C-3 -8 -2 0 3 6 C7 11 6 16 0 20" stroke="#6F4E37" stroke-width="2" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round"/></g></svg>

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a thick circular badge outline in deep red, giving the mark a stamped, traditional trattoria feel; behind the circle place a soft warm cream background disc for contrast; inside the circle draw a simple gabled roof arc in brown just above center to symbolize home and family; below the roof place a golden-yellow triangular pizza slice with a gently curved crust edge rendered in a darker toasted-brown outline; scatter three small red pepperoni circles and two tiny green herb flecks across the slice's surface for texture and appetite appeal; wrap a thin decorative rope-like border in dark red just inside the outer circle to reinforce the emblem, rustic-bakery style; keep all linework slightly imperfect and hand-drawn to convey warmth and authenticity rather than corporate precision.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><defs><radialGradient id="cream" cx="50%" cy="45%" r="60%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff6e3"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#f5e3c0"/></radialGradient></defs><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="104" fill="url(#cream)"/><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="103" fill="none" stroke="#9c1f1f" stroke-width="13"/><circle cx="127" cy="129" r="103" fill="none" stroke="#8a1414" stroke-width="2" opacity="0"/><g fill="none" stroke="#7a1414" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="6,4" opacity="0"><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="88"/></g><g fill="none" stroke="#6a1010" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2,6" opacity="0"><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="92"/></g><path d="M77,110 Q90,58 128,55 Q167,59 180,111" fill="none" stroke="#7a4a2a" stroke-width="9" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M83,109 Q92,66 128,63" fill="none" stroke="#8a5a35" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/><g><path d="M96,190 L128,110 L163,188 Q129,206 96,190 Z" fill="#f4b73d" stroke="#7a4a1f" stroke-width="5" stroke-linejoin="round"/><path d="M99,189 Q129,203 160,187" fill="none" stroke="#6b3d1a" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M101,183 Q129,196 158,181" fill="none" stroke="#c98f3f" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/></g><circle cx="117" cy="150" r="7" fill="#b5241f" stroke="#7d1712" stroke-width="1"/><circle cx="140" cy="140" r="6" fill="#b5241f" stroke="#7d1712" stroke-width="1"/><circle cx="131" cy="172" r="6" fill="#b5241f" stroke="#7d1712" stroke-width="1"/><g fill="#4f7a34" stroke="#3a5c26" stroke-width="0"><ellipse cx="122" cy="165" rx="4" ry="2" transform="rotate(30 122 165)"/><ellipse cx="146" cy="160" rx="3" ry="1" transform="rotate(-20 146 160)"/></g></svg>

- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

a soft circular frame rendered as a thin gold ring sits centered as the outer boundary, evoking a wedding band; behind it a pale blush-cream circle fills the background of the emblem to create warmth and softness; in the upper portion of the circle a delicate veil shape drapes downward, drawn as two flowing symmetrical curved lines that taper to points like fabric caught in a breeze, rendered in soft gold; at the center where the veil meets, a small elegant rose blossom is formed from a few overlapping curved petal strokes in a muted mauve tone, symbolizing the bride; a slender vertical line descends from the rose like a stem, ending in a tiny leaf flourish to add organic balance; the entire composition sits within generous negative space so the mark reads clean and luxurious at small sizes, with no lettering, only the veil, ring, and rose forming the elegant bridal emblem.

- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a soft rounded hexagon outline in deep navy (#1E3A5F) as the outer container, symbolizing a shared building or hub. Inside it, place three overlapping rounded-square 'desk' shapes, each slightly rotated and offset, in orange (#F2A93B), teal (#2FB6A3), and navy, representing individual workspaces or people. Where the three shapes overlap in the middle, blend the color into a small bright circle in off-white (#F7F5F1) to signify shared connection and community. Add a thin connecting line or two subtle dots between the shapes suggesting network links, drawn in the teal accent. Keep the background a warm off-white (#F7F5F1) for a clean, modern feel. Ensure the whole mark is symmetric and balanced, reading clearly as a small icon at any size.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><rect x="-9999" y="-9999" width="19998" height="19998" fill="#F7F5F1"/><defs><filter id="soft" x="-20%" y="-20%" width="140%" height="140%"><feDropShadow dx="0" dy="1" stdDeviation="1" flood-color="#1E3A5F" flood-opacity="0"/></filter></defs><polygon points="128,26 210,72 210,164 128,210 46,164 46,72" fill="none" stroke="#1E3A5F" stroke-width="10" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="98" y1="108" x2="128" y2="128" stroke="#2FB6A3" stroke-width="3" stroke-dasharray="1 7" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="158" y1="108" x2="128" y2="128" stroke="#2FB6A3" stroke-width="3" stroke-dasharray="1 7" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="128" y1="150" x2="128" y2="128" stroke="#2FB6A3" stroke-width="3" stroke-dasharray="1 7" stroke-linecap="round"/><g filter="url(#soft)"><rect x="92" y="98" width="56" height="56" rx="14" fill="#F2A93B" transform="rotate(-18 120 126)" opacity="0"/><rect x="110" y="98" width="56" height="56" rx="14" fill="#2FB6A3" transform="rotate(14 138 126)" opacity="0"/><rect x="101" y="112" width="56" height="56" rx="14" fill="#1E3A5F" transform="rotate(-3 129 140)" opacity="0"/></g><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="15" fill="#F7F5F1" stroke="#F7F5F1" stroke-width="1"/></svg>

A large rounded circle in deep navy (#0E2A3D) sits behind everything as a bold badge backdrop, giving weight and containment to the mark; inside it, a smooth teal-green (#2E7D6B) coffee bean silhouette is rotated diagonally and centered, its classic curved center seam rendered as a thin negative-space channel; overlapping the lower-right edge of the bean, a crisp faceted ice-crystal droplet shape in pale cream (#F4EDE0) juts outward, its geometric angular facets contrasting the bean's organic curve to signal 'cold'; a few small condensation droplets in soft light-teal (#B8DDD4) are scattered near the ice shape's base to reinforce chill and freshness; a thin cream ring traces just inside the navy circle's edge as a clean containing border, giving the whole mark a polished, modern badge finish suitable at both small and large scale.

Centered on a soft cream background, a large rounded map-pin silhouette in deep forest green forms the outer shape of the logo, its point anchored toward the bottom center; inside the pin, a lighter green double-peaked mountain sits in the upper portion, overlapping the pin's curve for depth; a winding dotted trail line in warm orange threads from the base of the pin up through a gap between the mountain peaks, symbolizing a hiking path leading to the summit; a small solid circle in orange caps the top end of the dotted trail like a destination marker or sun; a thin cream-colored outline traces the inner edge of the pin to separate it cleanly from the mountain shape, giving the whole mark a flat, iconic, single-color-friendly silhouette suitable for an app icon.'

A soft circular badge in deep navy fills the background, giving the mark a grounded, protective frame; inside the circle a gentle gradient horizon line separates a calm teal lower half from a warm dawn-orange upper half, symbolizing the passage from struggle to hope; centered on the horizon stands a simplified shield silhouette in muted navy, its outline echoing military service and safety; growing from within the shield is a minimal tree form with a strong trunk and rounded canopy rendered in teal, its roots extending downward and outward beneath the horizon line to suggest resilience and stability; a small radiant sun with a few short rays sits just above the tree's canopy in golden orange, peeking over the horizon to represent recovery and renewed hope; two thin curved lines sweep upward on either side of the shield like gentle wings or supporting arms, rendered in a lighter cream tone, reinforcing the idea of community support cradling the individual; the overall composition is symmetrical, clean, and iconic, designed to read clearly at small sizes on uniforms, letterhead, or a website favicon.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><defs><linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#f4a13a"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#f7c873"/></linearGradient><linearGradient id="sea" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#1f7f7a"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#14514f"/></linearGradient><clipPath fill="url(#sky)"/><rect x="16" y="128" width="224" height="104" fill="url(#sea)"/></g><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="104" fill="none" stroke="#0d1830" stroke-width="3" opacity="0"/><path d="M62 96 C 80 y1="88" x2="112" y2="93" stroke="#f4a13a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="150" y1="88" x2="144" y2="93" stroke="#f4a13a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/></g><path d="M128 108 C 108 118, 100 138, 104 158 C 108 180, 120 192, 128 200 C 136 192, 148 180, 152 158 C 156 138, 148 118, 128 108 Z" fill="#16283f" stroke="#0d1830" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M128 132 L128 178" A rounded shield silhouette in deep navy forms the outer frame, symbolizing protection and safety; inside the shield, a simple triangular house-roof shape in white sits at the base, representing homes and communities being safeguarded; above the roofline, three concentric arcs radiate outward in warm amber, like a signal or alert pulse, symbolizing early warning and preparedness; a small solid circle in teal sits at the peak where the arcs originate, acting as a beacon or alert source; the overall composition is centered and symmetrical, with clean geometric edges and generous negative space so the mark reads clearly at small sizes; the palette stays limited to navy, amber, teal, and white for a stable, optimistic, civic-minded feel.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><defs><linearGradient id="navyGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1"><stop offset="0" stop-color="#1b2a4a"/><stop offset="1" stop-color="#0f1c33"/></linearGradient></defs><path d="M128 22 L206 50 V128 C206 178 172 212 128 234 C84 212 50 178 50 128 V50 Z" fill="url(#navyGrad)" stroke="#0a1626" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M128 46 L192 68 V124 C192 166 164 194 128 212 C92 194 64 166 64 124 V68 Z" fill="none" stroke="#2c3e63" stroke-width="1" opacity="0"/><path d="M85 172 L128 132 L171 172 Z" fill="#ffffff"/><rect x="96" y="172" width="64" height="14" fill="#ffffff"/><path d="M128 96 A26 26 0 0 1 154 122" fill="none" stroke="#f2a541" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M128 96 A26 26 0 0 0 102 122" fill="none" stroke="#f2a541" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M128 82 A40 40 0 0 1 168 122" fill="none" stroke="#f2a541" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/><path d="M128 82 A40 40 0 0 0 88 122" fill="none" stroke="#f2a541" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/><path d="M128 68 A54 54 0 0 1 182 122" fill="none" stroke="#f2a541" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/><path d="M128 68 A54 54 0 0 0 74 122" fill="none" stroke="#f2a541" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/><circle cx="128" cy="96" r="10" fill="#1a9e96"/></svg>

Center a large circular emblem on a soft cream background; within the circle, a warm teal disc forms the base backdrop; behind the wheel, a bright orange sun sits low, half-hidden by a horizon line near the bottom third; three simple curved wave lines in lighter teal sweep across the lower part of the circle, suggesting shoreline surf; overlaying the sun and waves, a bold bicycle wheel outline in cream white sits centered, its spokes rendered as clean thin lines radiating from a small hub circle; the wheel's rim is drawn as a thick ring so it reads clearly at small sizes; a slender palm leaf silhouette in coral curves along the upper right inside the circle, adding a beach accent without cluttering the wheel; the whole composition is contained in a thin circular border in dark teal to frame the icon like a badge.

- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a soft blush-pink circular disc as the backdrop, warm and inviting like a fabric swatch. Inside it, place a single continuous curved line forming an elegant hourglass-plus-size female silhouette shown from the shoulders to hips in side profile, rendered in a deep charcoal line with generous, rounded curves that celebrate fullness rather than minimize it. Layer a thin gold arc behind the silhouette's shoulder, sweeping like a sash or ribbon, adding a touch of couture glamour. Add a small negative-space heart or flower shape cut cleanly where the waist curve meets the hip curve, formed naturally by the intersection of two curved strokes. Frame the whole mark with a delicate thin ring outline in the same charcoal tone, slightly inset from the edge of the disc, giving it a badge-like boutique emblem feel. Keep all linework smooth, confident, and rounded with no sharp angles, reinforcing softness, warmth, and body-positive elegance.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><defs><radialGradient id="blush" cx="50%" cy="45%" r="65%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fbe4e2"/><stop offset="55%" stop-color="#f7d3d0"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#f0bcc0"/></radialGradient><linearGradient id="goldArc" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#e8c477"/><stop offset="50%" stop-color="#d4a94f"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#c99a3d"/></linearGradient></defs><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="108" fill="url(#blush)"/><circle cx="128" cy="128" r="98" fill="none" stroke="#3a3230" stroke-width="1" opacity="0"/><path d="M 80 78 A 44 30 0 0 1 158 82" fill="none" stroke="url(#goldArc)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/><path d="M 110 62 C 100 66, 96 76, 100 86 C 84 92, 76 108, 82 124 C 74 138, 76 154, 90 164 C 84 178, 90 192, 106 198 C 124 205, 146 203, 158 190 C 170 178, 168 160, 154 150 C 166 138, 166 120, 152 108 C 158 96, 154 82, 140 76 C 148 70, 148 60, 138 55 C 128 50, 116 54, 110 62 Z" fill="none" stroke="#3a3230" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><path d="M 90 164 C 96 158, 106 158, 110 165 C 114 158, 124 158, 128 165 C 126 174, 116 182, 110 186 C 102 181, 92 173, 90 164 Z" fill="#f7d3d0"/></svg>

- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a thick circular badge outline in deep cocoa brown, giving the mark a stamped, handcrafted feel; fill the badge interior with warm cream background; inside, draw a plump waffle cone rendered as a simple triangle with crosshatch texture lines in golden tan pointing downward from center; atop the cone, stack two rounded scoop shapes in soft blush-orange and muted sage green, each with a gentle swirl line etched across its top to suggest hand-scooped texture; from the top scoop, sprout a small sprig of leaves in sage green leaning to one side, symbolizing an unusual herbal flavor infusion; scatter three tiny dot flecks in dark brown around the scoops to suggest mix-in specks like vanilla bean or cracked spice; finish with a thin arc of small dots tracing just inside the badge border, top and bottom, for a hand-stamped artisanal finish.

Centered on a soft cream circular badge background sits a rounded heart shape colored warm green, symbolizing care and safety; nestled inside and slightly overlapping the heart are two simplified animal head silhouettes facing each other nose-to-nose, one a dog with a floppy ear on the left in cream and one a cat with pointed ears on the right in orange, both cut out in negative space so the heart color shows through their outlines; a thin circular outline in dark charcoal frames the entire badge like a stamp or seal, giving it an official nonprofit feel; small rounded paw-print shapes in orange are scattered lightly along the bottom inner edge of the circle as a subtle decorative border; the overall composition is symmetrical, friendly, and simplified into flat clean shapes suitable for embroidery or small-scale use.

Center a warm dark-brown rounded shield background to give the mark a cozy, snackable badge feel; inside it place a bold red-and-white vertically striped popcorn box shape narrowing toward the bottom like a classic carton; above the box, cluster three to five puffy irregular cloud-like popcorn kernels in cream white with soft golden-yellow shading to suggest butter and fluffiness, overlapping so they read as one bursting pile; add a couple of smaller kernel puffs peeking over the box rim on the sides for extra volume; place one small golden starburst or motion-spark to the upper right of the pile to imply popping energy and freshness; finish with a thin cream-colored outline around the entire badge and box to keep the shapes crisp and logo-like at small sizes.

Center a rounded glass jar silhouette outlined in deep green, wide at the base and slightly tapered at the neck, evoking a kombucha fermentation vessel; inside the jar place a swirling concentric spiral in warm amber and gold tones representing the SCOBY culture and bubbling tea; layer a single stylized tea leaf shape, curved and simple, growing upward from the spiral's center in a darker green to symbolize freshness and botanicals; add three small dot bubbles rising along the right edge of the jar in light gold to suggest carbonation; draw a thin horizontal band across the jar's neck in mustard yellow, like a lid or label strap, giving a handcrafted bottled look; finish with a soft circular badge outline in muted green encircling the whole composition to unify it into a clean, iconic emblem suitable for a tea bar sign.

Start with a solid dark charcoal-brown circle background suggesting a grill drum or cast-iron plate; inside it, place a slightly lighter warm brown ring as a border to frame the emblem; across the lower third, draw simple bold horizontal grate lines in dark umber to evoke a barbecue grill surface; rising up from the center of the grate, sculpt a stylized flame shape in gradient orange-to-yellow (deep ember orange at the base fading to bright golden-yellow at the tip), with the flame split into two or three overlapping tongues for movement; behind and above the flame, add soft rounded smoke puff shapes in warm off-white or pale cream, layered subtly to suggest rising smoke without cluttering the flame; finish with a thin cream-colored circular outline hugging the outer edge of the whole badge to give it a classic emblem/stamp look suitable for a barbecue restaurant.

Centered on a warm cream background, a rounded earthenware bowl shape in deep toasted brown forms the base anchor of the mark; inside the bowl, a stylized rising sun in golden-orange sits just above the rim, its simple rounded rays fanning outward like toasted oat clusters; scattered around the sun are three small clustered dot shapes in dark brown and forest green representing nuts, seeds, and dried fruit pieces; a thin curved honey-drizzle line in amber swirls diagonally across the sun and bowl for texture and warmth; a slender leaf sprig in muted green tucks beside the bowl's rim to suggest natural, organic ingredients; the entire composition sits within a soft circular badge outline in warm brown to give the logo a stamped, wholesome, farmstand feel.
